title: Local desktop conversation
outcome: A user opens Dennett Desktop, creates or resumes a local project conversation, receives a streaming response from a replaceable agent runtime, can stop the active turn, and sees the authoritative session restored after the UI restarts.
non_goals: editing project files, reviewing diffs, running project tests or performing Git actions, which belong to M02
managed background Tasks, Runs, Action Inbox or Agent Radar, which belong to M03
shipping local-model or second-provider adapters before their credentials and runtimes are available
production installers, remote-device sync, voice, ambient interaction or external connectors

work_packages.title: Prove the subscription-authenticated Codex SDK path
work_packages.outcome: The Node adapter host contains a secret-safe Codex SDK canary and deterministic contract tests proving that the pinned SDK can stream a trivial turn and resume the same thread through a dedicated Codex-managed login to the owner's existing ChatGPT subscription without an API key.
non_goals: making live provider calls part of the normal CI gate
defining the final cross-process runtime protocol
desktop UI or Tauri integration
supporting providers other than Codex in this package

reads: ephemeral canary prompt and dedicated Codex-managed ChatGPT authentication session
writes: normalized canary evidence and ephemeral Codex thread state only

observability: canary reports SDK/runtime version, normalized event kinds, thread continuation and latency without prompt text, tokens or credentials
missing or non-ChatGPT authentication fails with an actionable diagnostic before model execution
rollback: remove the Codex SDK dependency, canary and adapter tests without changing core contracts or user data
notes: The owner explicitly selected ChatGPT subscription usage and authorized login on 2026-07-17; API-key auth is out of scope because it uses separate API billing.
The SDK consumes a dedicated Dennett canary login created by the official Codex ChatGPT flow; the normal Codex auth file is never read, linked or modified.
Tests, logs and runtime code never parse, copy or print token values; per-run hard links and all session state remain inside bounded disposable directories.
Installing the first M01 dependency exposed that repository JSON validation scanned ignored dependency trees; the bounded verifier repair validates tracked canonical JSON only.
Planning lifecycle records, generated test views and generated repository metadata are package-completion artifacts.

work_packages.title: Establish the M01 protocol epoch and generated clients
work_packages.outcome: The pre-production dennett.v1 scaffold is replaced by the canonical subsystem package layout, M01 System, Project and Session commands plus snapshot/delta watch contracts are generated deterministically for Rust and TypeScript, strict Buf lint has no grandfathered exceptions, and the one-time epoch migration explicitly retires DEBT-0001 and DEBT-0002 before any production client compiles.
non_goals: desktop, Node, Head or provider implementation
mobile, voice, external-effect or multi-device protocol surfaces
preserving the pre-M01 generated API as a public compatibility promise
hand-written transport DTOs outside canonical Protobuf sources

reads: M00 protocol scaffold, checker-owned compatibility baseline and canonical subsystem package rules
writes: canonical M01 Protobuf sources, generated clients and explicit protocol-epoch compatibility evidence

required_commands: mise exec -- uv run --project . --frozen python -m unittest tools.tests.test_protocol_codegen
mise exec -- uv run --project . --frozen python tools/protocol_codegen.py check
observability: migration evidence lists every retired package and generated public symbol family without user data
compatibility diagnostics identify source file, field or method and the approved epoch boundary
descriptor diagnostics identify exact service modes, field numbers, presence and oneof drift
rollback: revert the complete protocol epoch before a production consumer merges; never mix old and new generated packages
restore DEBT-0001 and DEBT-0002 records if the epoch is abandoned
notes: This package is the mandatory repayment trigger recorded by DEBT-0001 and DEBT-0002.
The change is intentionally breaking only for the non-production M00 scaffold and requires detached adversarial protocol review plus owner approval before merge.
The full M01 wire surface is bounded to handshake, bootstrap, system/project/session commands, cancellation, typed result-or-error responses and revisioned system/session watch frames.
Retiring DEBT-0001 exposed a planning-validator unit test coupled to that live debt record; WP-M01-002 also replaces it with an isolated synthetic fixture.

The gross diff budget was corrected from 2600 to 10000 after deterministic replacement of checked-in Rust and TypeScript clients accounted for about 6575 changed lines; the handwritten/source change remains below the original budget and was not simplified to optimize the counter.

The first remote protocol run exposed anonymous GitHub API rate limiting in buf-setup-action before any repository code ran; the bounded workflow repair passes the read-only job token to the action and is protected by the protocol workflow test.
The owner accepted ADR-006 and authorized merge on 2026-07-18 after the detached R3 closure review returned PASS.

work_packages.title: Approve the first Project Chat workbench screen
work_packages.outcome: A runnable renderer implements the owner-selected dark translucent workbench direction for one Project Chat screen, with Activity Rail, session/project sidebar, direct chat, compact bottom composer controls and a read-only auxiliary pane, and exposes material states through deterministic fixtures for owner review.
non_goals: real daemon or provider integration
working Git, diff, terminal or project-file mutations
Orchestrator, Inbox, Radar or managed-run screens
locking every future visual token or layout variation

reads: deterministic Project Chat fixture through a transport-neutral DennettClient fake
writes: presentation state, draft text and layout preferences only

observability: visual review artifact records viewport, state fixture and commit
UI status distinguishes cached, streaming, stopped, timed out and stale states without technical-log dependence
rollback: revert the renderer components and visual tokens while retaining the skeleton bridge
notes: The owner chose the first generated visual direction, requested more minimal composer settings at the bottom and explicitly rejected generated UX semantics as authority.
Canonical UX comes from specification 60 plus independent review; the image is visual reference only.
The auxiliary pane is read-only in M01; real review and Git actions remain M02.

The gross diff budget includes the generated pnpm lockfile expansion for the icon and accessibility-test dependencies; increasing it from 2200 to 3200 prevents visual quality or test depth from being traded against a mechanical line counter.

work_packages.title: Normalize streaming runtime continuation and control
work_packages.outcome: The provider-neutral agent runtime contract supports ordered streaming events, opaque continuation identity, deadline and scoped cancellation; a scripted fake and the Codex adapter pass the same conformance suite without provider types escaping the adapter boundary.
non_goals: provider routing or automatic model fallback
tool execution against project files
multi-agent orchestration
persisting conversations or exposing desktop IPC

reads: provider-neutral runtime request and adapter-local continuation handle
writes: ordered normalized runtime events and cancellation acknowledgement

observability: events carry runtime request, turn and provider correlation identifiers but not prompt content
cancel, timeout and malformed provider events are distinct terminal outcomes
rollback: retain the unary fake runtime path and revert additive streaming protocol fields before any durable consumer depends on them
notes: Codex is the only real runtime in M01, but every core-facing type remains provider-neutral.
Provider-specific event payloads stay inside services/adapter-host-node or explicit wire conversion modules.

work_packages.title: Persist local project sessions and revisioned watch state
work_packages.outcome: The embedded local Head owns a versioned durable project-session journal and exposes bootstrap snapshots plus monotonic deltas so completed, partial, cancelled and timed-out turns restore after process restart and a detected stream gap requires resync.
non_goals: full Memory Fabric retrieval, evidence extraction or semantic indexing
multi-device replication or Head promotion
production backup and encryption policy beyond the bounded development profile
desktop rendering or provider SDK integration

reads: canonical local project-session events and client watch cursor
writes: versioned embedded-Head session journal, snapshots and monotonic revisions

observability: session revisions, stream sequence and command correlation are observable without message content in metric labels
recovery reports migration, integrity and stale-cursor failures explicitly
rollback: export the development session journal, revert the adapter and restore the previous schema version in an isolated profile
never silently delete or reinterpret an existing session database
notes: This is the first durable schema in active development and therefore requires owner review before merge plus detached technical review.
Ordinary UI caches remain non-canonical; this package writes only through the embedded Head's Memory and session ports.

work_packages.title: Connect Tauri to the persistent Node over authenticated local IPC
work_packages.outcome: Dennett Desktop discovers or starts an independently owned dennett-node, completes a versioned authenticated Named Pipe handshake on Windows, obtains a bootstrap snapshot, forwards watch frames through a thin Tauri channel and reconnects without stopping Node when the window closes.
non_goals: production OS service installers for every platform
unauthenticated localhost HTTP fallback
domain logic inside Tauri commands
mobile transport or remote Head connectivity

reads: per-install Node endpoint metadata and authoritative bootstrap/watch frames
writes: short-lived authenticated UI session and presentation-facing channel frames only

observability: startup trace records discovery, handshake, bootstrap and watch subscription phases
Node absent, protocol mismatch and reconnect failures map to actionable UI-safe errors
rollback: disable the M01 IPC feature and restore the skeleton bridge without stopping or deleting Node data
notes: Windows Named Pipe is the first implementation; Unix Domain Socket support must remain a transport adapter, not a different application contract.
Closing or crashing the Tauri window never sends the explicit Stop Dennett on Device command.

work_packages.title: Qualify the complete local desktop conversation
work_packages.outcome: The approved Project Chat screen drives the real Node and embedded Head through a transport-neutral client, supports fake and Codex runtimes, displays streaming progress, scoped Stop and visible timeout, resyncs a forced gap, and restores the same authoritative session after UI restart with one joined trace.
non_goals: project file edits, diff approval or Git operations
background managed Runs or multi-agent execution
release installers and cross-platform certification
using exact natural-language output as a correctness assertion

reads: authoritative project-session snapshot, ordered watch frames and normalized runtime events
writes: project chat commands through dennett-node and local presentation preferences

observability: one trace joins installation, component, project, session, command, runtime turn, provider and memory event identifiers
prompts and model responses remain out of metric labels and secret-safe diagnostics
rollback: switch the runtime binding to the deterministic fake and disable the desktop integration flag while preserving the durable session journal
notes: Milestone acceptance requires a live owner demo after automated qualification.
Codex live failure degrades the provider canary but does not invalidate deterministic core tests.

owner_acceptance: Project Chat feels like a direct coding-agent conversation rather than infrastructure management
Stop, active runtime and project scope are understandable without opening diagnostics
The chosen visual system remains attractive and minimal in the real first screen
Closing and reopening the desktop window restores the same session while Node remains alive
Timeout, cancellation and stale/resync states are visible and honest
Codex uses the owner's ChatGPT subscription login and no API-billed key is required
